Why it matters: Two spellings appeared in the prior pack (`D1ON` and `D1on`). A rebuilder needs one canonical ID for hashing, config, and audit traceability.
Evidence from prior spec: Section 1 used `Phase1ParentCore_ATRstandard_REVon_D1ON`; reference trace and Tier-2 trade log used `Phase1ParentCore_ATRstandard_REVon_D1on`.
Chosen resolution: Strategy name = VP1 (VTREND-P1). Legacy executable ID = `Phase1ParentCore_ATRstandard_REVon_D1ON`. `...D1on` is retained only as a historical alias seen in logs.

Why it matters: The prior spec was mostly explicit, but several critical rules still leaned on lineage language (`inferred from Baseline lineage`) rather than being promoted into first-class executable rules.
Evidence from prior spec: Fast-period rule, ATR20/Wilder, VDO EMA 12/28, warmup=365d, D1/VDO entry-only, reversal priority, anomaly semantics were all justified from lineage.
Chosen resolution: Promote every inherited rule into explicit standalone VP1 rules in v1.1. VP1 must be rebuildable without reading Baseline.

Why it matters: Without an exact rule, a rebuilder can produce different entry eligibility on bars where taker data is missing only on some bars or fallback denominator is zero.
Evidence from prior spec: The prior pack defined primary and fallback VDR paths, but did not fully lock whether current-bar VDO becomes NaN or remains finite via EMA carry on partial-missing bars.
Chosen resolution: Per-bar auto path is canonical. If `taker_buy_base_vol` is finite and `volume > 0`, use primary VDR. Else use fallback VDR if `high > low`; otherwise current `vdr = NaN`. VDO uses `EMA_nan_carry`, so if VDR is NaN and the EMA has already been seeded, current VDO remains equal to the carried EMA spread. If the EMA has never been seeded, current VDO remains NaN.

Why it matters: A rebuilder must not confuse a benchmark-window forced flatten with an intrinsic strategy exit. If they do, live behavior and finite-window backtest behavior will diverge.
Evidence from prior spec: The prior pack mentioned `window_flatten` but did not separate strategy logic from benchmark-wrapper logic sharply enough.
Chosen resolution: VP1 has **no intrinsic EOF exit**. In open-ended operation, a position remains open until a normal exit fires. In finite benchmark windows only, the wrapper may apply a synthetic `window_flatten` sell at the final allowed valuation open. That wrapper event is not part of strategy logic.

Why it matters: A rebuilder needs to know whether 50 bps round-trip is part of the strategy spec or part of the benchmark harness. Blurring the two changes accounting and makes code non-portable.
Evidence from prior spec: The prior pack stated the 50 bps headline cost model explicitly, but did not isolate clearly enough that this is the canonical benchmark-harness cost assumption, not a proprietary strategy rule.
Chosen resolution: Canonical rebuild separates strategy from harness. VP1 logic is cost-agnostic. Canonical benchmark reproduction uses flat 50 bps round-trip = 25 bps per side, charged exactly at BUY/SELL events under event accounting.

Field / rule: Acceptance tests using non-artifact full-history timestamps
Why it matters: A test suite is not executable if expected values are not preserved anywhere in the supplied artifact stack.
Evidence from prior spec: The prior pack listed full-history first legal trade timestamps and first few entries/exits as `INFERRED` from an unpreserved run, not from a shipped artifact.
Chosen resolution: Remove those as hard acceptance gates. Canonical acceptance tests must use artifact-backed Tier-2 expected values, the VP1 spec hash, and deterministic formula/unit tests. Full-history timestamps are demoted to non-binding historical notes.

Why it matters: A rebuilder may incorrectly treat mixed-source indicator values as frozen truth.
Evidence from prior spec: The prior trace combined real Tier-2 trade timestamps with indicator values described as recomputed from canonical formulas, but those recomputed numbers were not included as a preserved artifact.
Chosen resolution: Demote the old mixed trace to an illustrative trace only. The canonical frozen trace for acceptance is the real Tier-2 trade-log cycle (times, prices, reasons, NAV fields). Indicator-number verification remains local recomputation, not artifact truth.

Why it matters: The prior pack selected hard-fail behavior for duplicates, gaps, and invalid prices, but did not label those choices as forced resolutions.
Evidence from prior spec: Duplicate timestamps, structural gaps, negative prices, and absent bars were resolved operationally but not tagged distinctly.
Chosen resolution: Keep hard-fail policy and relabel it as `FORCED-RESOLUTION` wherever the artifact stack does not directly specify behavior.
